text.title: django-pytest-mocking Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for django-pytest-mocking as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Mocking at the definition site instead of the import site: @patch("stripe.Charge.create") when the service uses import stripe.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Python binds names at import time. The service's local name apps.payments.services.stripe is already bound. Patching the original stripe module does not affect the bound name in the service. Always patch at the import site.

failure-mode.description: Mocking Django ORM methods (objects.filter, objects.create, .save) instead of using real test data.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: ORM mocks bypass the database entirely, making tests that assert on "data was saved" meaningless. Use the db fixture and real factories. Real database tests with savepoint rollback are fast enough and catch real bugs.

failure-mode.description: Mocking time.sleep to speed up rate-limit tests or retry loops.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Mocking sleep changes test behavior: the retry logic no longer sleeps, but the rate-limiter state machine may not work correctly without real delays. Patch the rate-limiter or retry mechanism directly, not the sleep primitive.

failure-mode.description: Tests computing datetime.now() at module load time for comparison values: EXPIRY = datetime.now() + timedelta(days=7) at module level.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Module-level datetime.now() calls are evaluated once when the module is collected, not per test. The value drifts across long test runs. Move time calculations inside the test function or use freeze_time to pin the clock.

failure-mode.description: Not verifying that a mock was called, only asserting on the return value it produced.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: If the mock is not called but returns a value anyway (because the code path was skipped), the test passes with no real verification. Always use assert_called_once_with (or assert_called) to verify the call happened with the expected arguments.
